judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, the agent imposed an arbitrary "significance" threshold (top month count ≥ second month count + 5) and returned null, even though the data’s peak month (which should be November) exists, thus adding an unnecessary constraint not required by the query.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent’s final computation used a Case query that only required orderitemid__c IS NOT NULL (var_call_PIVr6arERlNpRogdhDQhvj1a) and then applied a 1.5x “significance” threshold in Python (var_call_Rg6EDcm9jgNirf9jE7vwXhax), omitting the required filter for the specified product ID, so it answered for all products and returned None instead of the correct month (November).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the contributing query (var_call_ceosBXadmXdRhKJbky6daGkZ), the agent filtered support.Case by orderitemid__c = '01tWt000006hVJdIAM'—a Product2 Id—using the wrong field and yielding an empty result, leading it to falsely conclude no month stood out.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered support Case records by equating Case.orderitemid__c to the provided Product2 Id and never joined through OrderItem to Product2, so its contributing queries returned empty results and it incorrectly concluded there was no standout month.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried sales_pipeline.OpportunityLineItem to get IDs and matched them against support.Case.orderitemid__c (call_jn9OfrFI7WXB3zSxfTXZjLA6 and call_d8EVEMzOZLKLdkbkK61gRmOv), but orderitemid__c corresponds to OrderItem in products_orders, so the query returned no rows and led to the incorrect “no month” conclusion.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

FM3: yes
FM4: no
